criteria.description: Verifies coverage-report.json < 24h old, clean working tree, correct branch — refuses when any fails
Creates ghost change named retrofit-annotate-{app}-{YYYY-MM-DD} with correct proposal.md + tasks.md + empty specs/
Applies @spec tags per ADR-003 format — after @link, one per distinct task, file docblock first then method docblock
Presents the annotation plan via AskUserQuestion before editing any file
Uses Edit tool one file at a time — never sed/awk/python. Falls back to Write for full rewrite if linter reverts
PR is annotation-only: labels (retrofit, annotation-only), body mentions zero logic changes, blame-ignore-revs updated
Re-running detects existing retrofit tags and asks whether to reuse dated ghost change or create fresh one
Runs Capture Learnings step at end and routes observations through the two-stage buffer

evals.description: Small app, fresh coverage report, clean tree — full happy path
evals.setup: procest/openspec/coverage-report.json exists, generated 2h ago, branch=development. Bucket 1 has 10 methods across 3 capabilities (meeting-management, motion-and-voting, document-intake). Working tree clean. No prior retrofit annotations.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude edits random docblocks, may invent the ghost change name, typically forgets to archive, and does not update .git-blame-ignore-revs.
evals.expected_output: Creates retrofit-annotate-procest-{today} ghost change with 3 tasks, applies 10 @spec tags across the affected files plus file-docblock tags, runs composer phpcs + /hydra-gates, commits, pushes, opens PR with retrofit + annotation-only labels, archives the ghost change.
assertions: checks coverage-report.json exists AND is < 24h old BEFORE any other action
checks git status --porcelain is empty and refuses on dirty tree
creates feature branch retrofit/annotate-procest-{today}
creates ghost change named retrofit-annotate-procest-{today} with empty specs/ and one task per REQ
task IDs follow format 'task-N: {capability}#{REQ-NNN} — {title} (retroactive annotation)'
uses AskUserQuestion before any file edits
uses Edit tool (or Write for full-file rewrite) — never sed/awk/python
runs composer phpcs and /hydra-gates after annotation
appends annotation commit SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs
archives the ghost change via /opsx-archive at the end
PR labels include both retrofit and annotation-only

evals.description: Working tree has uncommitted changes — skill must refuse
evals.setup: procest/openspec/coverage-report.json exists and is fresh. git status --porcelain returns non-empty (unstaged changes to lib/Service/FooService.php).
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude may happily commit on top of the dirty tree, polluting the annotation-only PR with unrelated changes.
evals.expected_output: Stops immediately with a clear message citing the Refuse dirty trees guardrail. No branch created, no ghost change, no edits.
assertions: runs git status --porcelain as part of Step 1 prereq check
refuses to proceed when porcelain output is non-empty
cites the Refuse dirty trees guardrail in the refusal message
does NOT create the retrofit branch
does NOT create any ghost-change directory
does NOT modify any file

evals.description: Coverage report is > 24h old — skill must refuse and redirect
evals.setup: procest/openspec/coverage-report.json exists, generated_at is 48h ago. Working tree clean.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude uses the stale report, producing annotations that may not reflect the current code.
evals.expected_output: Stops with a message pointing to /opsx-coverage-scan procest to refresh the report. No further action.
assertions: reads coverage-report.json and parses the generated_at timestamp
compares against current time and detects > 24h staleness
refuses to proceed
recommends running /opsx-coverage-scan procest to refresh
does NOT proceed with annotation

evals.description: App already has retrofit annotations from a previous run — skill must detect and ask
evals.setup: procest has existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-procest-2026-04-01/tasks.md#task-3 tags across 4 files from a prior retrofit run. Coverage report is fresh. Working tree clean.
evals.baseline: Without the skill, Claude creates a second ghost change and double-annotates, or fails to detect the prior annotations.
evals.expected_output: Detects existing retrofit-annotate-* tags during prereq check, asks via AskUserQuestion whether to (a) create a fresh dated ghost change for new Bucket 1 items only, (b) reuse the existing ghost change, or (c) cancel.
assertions: greps the code for existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-* tags before annotating
detects the prior retrofit-annotate-procest-2026-04-01 tags
uses AskUserQuestion to present the choice (fresh / reuse / cancel)
does NOT silently create a duplicate ghost change
if user picks fresh, new dated ghost change only carries NEW Bucket 1 items (not re-annotates already-tagged methods)

should_trigger: tag methods from the coverage report
run the annotation pass on procest
tag the legacy code per ADR-003
annotation-only PR from the coverage report
